question.text: For listed REIT units on which STT is paid, what is the LTCG tax rate if the units are held for more than 12 months?

answer.text: Exempt from tax if held for more than 12 months

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For listed REIT units where STT is paid, LTCG (holding period >12 months) is taxed at 12.5% on gains exceeding Rs. 1,25,000 under Section 112A. The STCG rate (holding period <=12 months) is 20% under Section 111A. **The Trap:** "10% above Rs. 1,00,000" was the old rate before it was revised. Many study materials and candidates still remember the previous threshold. The updated rate is 12.5% above Rs. 1,25,000. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) also tests LTCG on listed equity instruments; the same Section 112A applies to equity mutual funds, listed shares, and listed REIT/InvIT units. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Rs. 1,25,000 exemption is per taxpayer per year across ALL assets covered under Section 112A (equity shares, equity MF units, REIT units, InvIT units). Advise clients to plan redemptions to stay within this annual threshold.
